generatedSentence: Kael, an aging and weary replicant operator, meticulously logged a notch on his forearm, a grim number marking another solitary journey through the brown dust-choked wasteland. He used old hotkeys to navigate his rover, a rickety vehicle with a cracked windscreen, relying on salvaged electronic gear and a constantly crackling radio for faint signals. His vocation as an outsider involved research into pre-cataclysmic data, the remnants of a vanished popular world. He kept a small, worn bookmark in a historical text, a silent testament to a life filled with nostalgia for something he never experienced, often sketching graphic notes and writing down his observations in chalk on a metal panel. His keen intellect and synthetic capability often felt wasted. He often felt a faint jingle of unease, a constant, low-level voltage in his synthetic being, anticipating unseen ramifications. He hoped for optimism, but reality offered only a mundane existence. His odyssey was suddenly interrupted. A cloud of dust on the horizon signaled hostile riders, a flocks of scavengers who often injures any pilgrim in their path. Kael gripped the steering wheel, his synthetic eyebrows furrowed. He had hypothesized such encounters, but this time they were numerous, moving with dangerous velocity. A desperate emergency flared, and he had no desire to flail blindly. He knew the holocaust that had ravaged the old world had left behind wacky survivors, and these monkeys – as he contemptuously called them – often carried crude weapons. He’d heard reviews of their brutal tactics, capable of turning an area into an instant hospital scene. He was waiting for the right moment, ready to dig into his reserve of hidden dynamite. Thankfully, he had prepared for such a confrontation, a ging for his inevitable escape, a glimmer of paradise through the chaos. The rebellion of scavengers closed in, their crude aircraft – repurposed drones – buzzing overhead. Kael, remembering his uncle’s old combat stories, remained surprisingly orderly. He knew he needed to neutralize the threat swiftly. He activated a hidden switch, feeling a surge of joules as his rover's defenses activated. He had a licence to kill, a grim necessity in this new world. As one large gawky rider, resembling a prehistoric mammoth in bulk, approached, Kael jousted his reinforced bumper against the assailant's vehicle. He had to be factual in his assessment of their strength, aware of the infinity of dangers that lurked. He quickly deployed a blanket of smoke, hoping to illuminate their vulnerabilities and enable his escape. He quickly worked his hotkeys, preparing to detonate the dynamite. His mission was simple: survive and complete his undercover task. The explosions rattled the landscape, creating a brief firefly display of sparks and debris. Kael, though his systems registered minor damage, felt a peculiar sense of achievement. He took a moment to buckle his safety harness, securing his body for the jarring ride ahead. The immediate ramification of his actions would likely be further pursuit, but he had bought himself precious time. He knew this wasn't a normal way to live, but survival demanded constant adaptation. He checked his vital functions, ensuring proper nutrition and oxygen flow, a form of self-therapy. He even hummed a forgotten tune, a flicker of boohoo nostalgia for things like candy and waffle from before the great cataclysm. He often thought of old murals depicting a forgotten past, perhaps a planetarium or a peaceful holiday world. His journey continued, an untying from the chaos, ever alert, ever focused on the next keyword in his encrypted search for a better future, always with an eye to trading what he found for knowledge. He even allowed himself a brief, wacky thought of a pickpocket trying to swipe his tech, a remnant of forgotten nightlife.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, a gritty sci-fi graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Kael, a grizzled, middle-aged male replicant with visible facial circuitry scars, wearing worn, heavy-duty scavenger gear and a dusty, tattered trench coat. All human characters are visibly adult with mature features. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Kael, a grizzled, middle-aged male replicant, inside his dusty, worn rover, gazing intently out the cracked windscreen at a vast, desolate brown wasteland, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Distant, menacing figures of multiple adult male riders on rugged vehicles, brandishing crude weapons, kicking up dust as they approach Kael's rover, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, with a determined expression, his hand on a control panel inside his rover, as an explosion erupts behind a charging rider's vehicle outside the window, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, with a weary yet resolute look, piloting his rover away from the smoking aftermath of the confrontation, looking over his shoulder at the receding dust clouds, eye-level tracking shot. Rendered with cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue, NO children.

definitions.definition: A V-shaped cut.
An indentation.
A mountain pass; a defile.
A discontinuous change in a taxation schedule.
A level or degree.
definitions.example: Can you speak a notch louder, please?

definitions.definition: A portion of a mobile phone that overlaps the edge of the screen, used to house camera, sensors etc. while maximizing screen space.
To cut a notch in (something).
To record (a score or similar) by making notches on something.
definitions.example: The tribe's hunters notch their kills by notches on each's axe's handle.

definitions.definition: To join by means of notches.
To achieve (something); to add to one's score or record of successes.
definitions.example: The team notched a pair of shutout wins on Sunday.

definitions.definition: To fit (an arrow) to a bow by means of the notch cut at the end of the arrow; to nock.
To plan, control and record the position and course of a vehicle, ship, aircraft, etc., on a journey; to follow a planned course.
definitions.example: He navigated the bomber to the Ruhr.

definitions.definition: To give directions, as from a map, to someone driving a vehicle.
definitions.example: You drive. I'll navigate.

definitions.definition: To travel over water in a ship; to sail.
definitions.example: We navigated to France in the dinghy.

definitions.definition: To move between web pages, menus, etc. by means of hyperlinks, mouse clicks, or any other mechanism.
definitions.example: It was difficult to navigate back to the home page.

definitions.definition: To find a way through a difficult situation or process.
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
definitions.example: Zero, one, −1, 2.5, and pi are all numbers.

definitions.definition: A numeral: a symbol for a non-negative integer.
definitions.example: The number 8 is usually made with a single stroke.

definitions.definition: An element of one of several sets: natural numbers, integers, rational numbers, real numbers, complex numbers, and sometimes extensions such as hypercomplex numbers, etc.
definitions.example: The equation e^{i\pi}+1=0 includes the most important numbers: 1, 0, \pi, i, and e.

definitions.definition: (Followed by a numeral; used attributively) Indicating the position of something in a list or sequence. Abbreviations: No or No., no or no. (in each case, sometimes written with a superscript "o", like Nº or №). The symbol "#" is also used in this manner.
definitions.example: Horse number 5 won the race.

definitions.definition: Quantity.
definitions.example: Any number of people can be reading from a given repository at a time.

definitions.definition: A sequence of digits and letters used to register people, automobiles, and various other items.
definitions.example: Her passport number is C01X864TN.

definitions.definition: A telephone number.
(grammar) Of a word or phrase, the state of being singular, dual or plural, shown by inflection.
definitions.example: Adjectives and nouns should agree in gender, number, and case.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Poetic metres; verses, rhymes.
A performance; especially, a single song or song and dance routine within a larger show.
definitions.example: For his second number, he sang "The Moon Shines Bright".

definitions.definition: A person.
An item of clothing, particularly a stylish one.
A marijuana cigarette, or joint; also, a quantity of marijuana bought form a dealer.
An issue of a periodical publication.
definitions.example: the latest number of a magazine

definitions.definition: A large amount, in contrast to a smaller amount; numerical preponderance.
To label (items) with numbers; to assign numbers to (items).
definitions.example: Number the baskets so that we can find them easily.

definitions.definition: To total or count; to amount to.
definitions.example: I don’t know how many books are in the library, but they must number in the thousands.

definitions.definition: A strip of material used to mark a place in a book.
A record of the address of a file or Internet page serving as a shortcut to it.
A pointer found in a nonclustered index to a row in a clustered index or a table heap
To create a bookmark.
A colour like that of chocolate or coffee.
definitions.example: The browns and greens in this painting give it a nice woodsy feel.

definitions.definition: One of the colour balls used in snooker, with a value of 4 points.
Black tar heroin.
A copper coin.
A brown horse or other animal.
(sometimes capitalised) A person of Middle Eastern, Latino or South Asian descent; a brown-skinned person; someone of mulatto or biracial appearance.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of subfamily Satyrinae (formerly the family Satyridae).
Any of certain species of nymphalid butterflies of subfamily Satyrinae, such as those of the genera Heteronympha and Melanitis.
A brown trout (Salmo trutta).
To become brown.
definitions.example: Fry the onions until they brown.

definitions.definition: To cook something until it becomes brown.
definitions.example: Brown the onions in a large frying pan.

definitions.definition: To tan.
definitions.example: Light-skinned people tend to brown when exposed to the sun.

definitions.definition: To make brown or dusky.
To give a bright brown colour to, as to gun barrels, by forming a thin coating of oxide on their surface.
(usually derogatory) To turn progressively more Middle Eastern, Hispanic or Latino, in the context of the population of a geographic region.
definitions.example: the browning of America

definitions.definition: To treat with deference, or respect.
Having a brown colour.
Gloomy.
(sometimes capitalized) Of or relating to any of various ethnic groups having dark pigmentation of the skin.
A tool used for threshing, consisting of a long handle with a shorter stick attached with a short piece of chain, thong or similar material.
A weapon which has the (usually spherical) striking part attached to the handle with a flexible joint such as a chain.
To beat using a flail or similar implement.
To wave or swing vigorously
To thresh.
To move like a flail.
definitions.example: He was flailing wildly, but didn't land a blow.

definitions.definition: Someone thought to have an unusual degree of enlightenment.
To shine light on something.
To decorate something with lights.
To clarify or make something understandable.
To decorate the page of a manuscript book with ornamental designs.
To make spectacular.
To glow; to light up.
To be exposed to light.
To direct a radar beam toward.
Enlightened
A soft, white, powdery limestone.
A piece of chalk, or nowadays processed compressed gypsum, that is used for drawing and for writing on a blackboard.
Tailor's chalk.
A white powdery substance used to prevent hands slipping from holds when climbing, sometimes but not always limestone-chalk.
A platoon-sized group of airborne soldiers.
(horseracing) The favorite in a sporting event.
The prediction that there will be no upsets, and the favored competitor will win.
To apply chalk to anything, such as the tip of a billiard cue.
To record something, as on a blackboard, using chalk.
To use powdered chalk to mark the lines on a playing field.
To record a score or event, as if on a chalkboard.
To manure (land) with chalk.
To make white, as if with chalk; to make pale; to bleach.
Edible, sweet-tasting confectionery containing sugar, or sometimes artificial sweeteners, and often flavored with fruit, chocolate, nuts, herbs and spices, or artificial flavors.
A piece of confectionery of this kind.
(slang, chiefly US) crack cocaine.
To cook in, or coat with, sugar syrup.
To have sugar crystals form in or on.
definitions.example: Fruits preserved in sugar candy after a time.

definitions.definition: To be formed into candy; to solidify in a candylike form or mass.
Graphism of symbols such as letters that express some meaning.
Something written, such as a document, article or book.
The process of representing a language with symbols or letters.
A work of an author.
The style of writing of a person.
definitions.example: I can't read your writing.

definitions.definition: (as a modifier) Intended for or used in writing.
definitions.example: a writing table

definitions.definition: The sound of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: He heard the jingle of her keys in the door and turned off the screen.

definitions.definition: A small piece of metal attached to a musical instrument, such as a tambourine, so as to make a jangling sound when the instrument is played.
definitions.example: Her tambourine didn't come with any jingles attached.

definitions.definition: A memorable short song, or in some cases a snippet of a popular song with its lyrics modified, used for the purposes of advertising a product or service in a TV or radio commercial.
definitions.example: That used-car dealership's jingle has been stuck in my head since we heard that song.

definitions.definition: A carriage drawn by horses.
A brief phone call; a ring.
definitions.example: Give me a jingle when you find out something.

definitions.definition: A jingle shell.
Coin money.
To make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: The beads jingled as she walked.

definitions.definition: To cause to make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: She jingled the beads as she walked.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
A class of explosives made from nitroglycerine in an absorbent medium such as kieselguhr, used in mining and blasting; invented by Alfred Nobel in 1867.
A stick of trinitrotoluene (TNT)
Anything exceptionally dangerous, exciting or wonderful.
To blow up with dynamite or other high explosive.
A sacrifice that is completely burned to ashes.
Extensive destruction of a group (usually of people or animals), whether by deliberate agency or by natural agency (especially fire).
definitions.example: 1895 September 10, "ANOTHER ARMENIAN HOLOCAUST; Five Villages Burned, Five Thousand Persons Made Homeless, and Anti-Christians Organized.", in The New York Times

definitions.definition: In particular, a state-sponsored mass murder of an ethnic group, especially the Holocaust (which see).
To sacrifice and burn (an animal) completely.
To destroy completely, especially by fire.
(possibly nonstandard) To subject to a holocaust (mass annihilation); to destroy en masse. (Compare genocide.)
A longing for home or familiar surroundings; homesickness.
A bittersweet yearning for the things of the past.
Reminiscence of the speaker's childhood or younger years.
A vector quantity that denotes the rate of change of position with respect to time, or a speed with the directional component.
Rapidity of motion.
The rate of occurrence.
The number of times that an average unit of currency is spent during a specific period of time.
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
definitions.example: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.

definitions.definition: A branching-out, the act or result of developing branches; specifically the divergence of the stem and limbs of a plant into smaller ones, or of similar developments in blood vessels, anatomical structures etc.
An offshoot of a decision, fact etc.; a consequence or implication, especially one which complicates a situation.
An arrangement of branches.
One who rides, often on a horse or a motorcycle.
A provision annexed to a bill under the consideration of a legislature, having little connection with the subject matter of the bill.
(by extension) Something extra or burdensome that is imposed.
An amendment or addition to an entertainer's performance contract, often covering a performer's equipment or food, drinks, and general comfort requirements.
An additional benefit attached to an insurance contract.
A small, sliding piece of aluminium on a chemical balance, used to determine small weights.
An agent who goes out with samples of goods to obtain orders; a commercial traveller.
One who breaks in or manages a horse.
The first Lenormand card, also known as either the horseman or the cavalier.
A problem of extra difficulty added to another on an examination paper.
An old Dutch gold coin with the figure of a man on horseback stamped upon it.
Rock material in a vein of ore, dividing it.
An interior rib occasionally fixed in a ship's hold, reaching from the keelson to the beams of the lower deck, to strengthen the frame.
The second tier of casks in a vessel's hold.
A small forked weight which straddles the beam of a balance, along which it can be moved in the manner of the weight on a steelyard.
A robber.
A piece, such as the rook or bishop, which moves any distance in one direction, as long as no other piece is in the way.
Diligent inquiry or examination to seek or revise facts, principles, theories, applications, etc.; laborious or continued search after truth.
definitions.example: The research station that houses Wang and his team is outside Lijiang, a city of about 1.2 million people.

definitions.definition: A particular instance or piece of research.
To search or examine with continued care; to seek diligently.
To make an extensive investigation into.
To search again.
To place abreast, or in a line.
To have a ranking.
definitions.example: Their defense ranked third in the league.

definitions.definition: To assign a suitable place in a class or order; to classify.
To take rank of; to outrank.
One’s relative placement in a list.
(in combination) Having a specified rank.
Superior in rank.
The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.
A device that can capture (receive) the signal sent over radio waves and render the modulated signal as sound.
On-board entertainment system in a car, usually including a radio receiver as well as the capability to play audio from recorded media.
A device that can transmit radio signals.
The continuous broadcasting of sound via the Internet in the style of traditional radio.
To use two-way radio to transmit (a message) (to another radio or other radio operator).
definitions.example: I think the boat is sinking; we'd better radio for help. / I radioed him already. / Radio the coordinates this time. / OK. I radioed them the coordinates.

definitions.definition: To order or assist (to a location), using telecommunications.
A mammal, Capra aegagrus hircus, and similar species of the genus Capra.
A lecherous man.
A scapegoat.
A Pontiac GTO car.
(speech recognition) A person who is not easily understood by a speech recognition system; contrasted with sheep.
A fool, loser, or object of ridicule.
To allow goats to feed on.
To scapegoat.
: Of or pertaining to an electron or electrons.
Operating on the physical behavior of electrons, especially in semiconductors.
Generated by an electronic device.
definitions.example: electronic music

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to the Internet.
One who operates.
A telecommunications facilitator whose job is to establish temporary network connections.
A function or other mapping that carries variables defined on a domain into another variable or set of variables in a defined range.
The game of Chinese whispers.
A person who is adept at making deals or getting results, especially one who uses questionable methods.
A member of a military special operations unit.
The administrator of a channel or network on IRC.
A symbol that represents a construct in a programming language and differs from a normal function in its syntax.
A kind of expression that enters into an a-bar movement dependency and is said to bind a variable.
definitions.example: In the sentence "What did Bill say he wants to buy?", "what" is an operator, binding a phonetically empty variable.

definitions.definition: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome
definitions.example: I love her youth, her beauty and above all her optimism that everything will turn out fine.

definitions.definition: The doctrine that this world is the best of all possible worlds
The belief that good will eventually triumph over evil
One who is not part of a community or organization.
definitions.example: While the initiated easily understand the symbols, they are wholly inaccessible to outsiders.

definitions.definition: A newcomer with little or no experience in an organization or community.
definitions.example: Seeing the mess professional politicians have made of things is it any wonder the electorate is beginning to prefer outsiders.

definitions.definition: A competitor or contestant who has little chance of winning; a long shot
definitions.example: Johnny was an outsider at this years karate tournament, but he still managed to win second place out of sheer determination.

definitions.definition: In a thankful manner; giving thanks.
(sometimes proscribed) fortunately, gratefully.
definitions.example: I was almost late for work, but thankfully there wasn't much traffic on the roads.

definitions.definition: To delay movement or action until the arrival or occurrence of; to await. (Now generally superseded by “wait for”.)
To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.
definitions.example: Wait here until your car arrives.

definitions.definition: To wait tables; to serve customers in a restaurant or other eating establishment.
definitions.example: She used to wait down at the Dew Drop Inn.

definitions.definition: To attend on; to accompany; especially, to attend with ceremony or respect.
To attend as a consequence; to follow upon; to accompany.
To defer or postpone (especially a meal).
definitions.example: to wait dinner

definitions.definition: To remain chaste while one's lover is unavailable.
Watching.
The act of staying or remaining in expectation.
Attendance, service.
A region with no remaining resources; a desert.
definitions.example: Ten years of drought had left the area a wasteland.

definitions.definition: Any barren or uninteresting place.
definitions.example: After his experiences, he no longer found western Kansas such a wasteland.

definitions.definition: The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
A great or heroic deed or feat; something accomplished by valor or boldness
An escutcheon or ensign armorial; now generally applied to the funeral shield commonly called hatchment.
An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.
definitions.example: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

definitions.definition: (grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in an instant.
An unremarkable, ordinary human being.
(in various subcultures) A person considered to be "normal", part of the mainstream culture, outside the subculture, not part of the elite group.
The world outside fandom; the normal, mainstream world.
(satanism) A person not a Satanist.
Worldly, earthly, profane, vulgar as opposed to heavenly.
Pertaining to the Universe, cosmos or physical reality, as opposed to the spiritual world.
Ordinary; not new.
Tedious; repetitive and boring.
A display museum in which images of stars and other astronomical phenomena are projected onto a domed ceiling.
An orrery.
One who steals from the pocket of a passerby, usually by sleight of hand.
To pick pockets; to steal.
The place where sanctified souls are believed to live after death.
definitions.example: Living in paradise comes with a price.

definitions.definition: (Abrahamic religions) A garden where Adam and Eve first lived after being created.
A very pleasant place; a place full of lush vegetation.
definitions.example: an island paradise in the Caribbean; a shoppers’ paradise

definitions.definition: A very pleasant experience.
An open space within a monastery or adjoining a church, such as the space within a cloister, the open court before a basilica, etc.
A churchyard or cemetery.
The upper gallery in a theatre.
To place (as) in paradise.
To transform into a paradise.
To affect or exalt with visions of happiness.

Common among the general public; generally accepted.

Cultivating the favour of the common people.
Liked by many people; generally pleasing, widely admired.

A place of lodging.
Hospitable.

A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
A keyboard shortcut.
Nocturnal activities, especially visiting nightclubs.

definitions.definition: The replacement of stale or noxious air with fresh.
The mechanical system used to circulate and replace air.
An exchange of views during a discussion.
The public exposure of an issue or topic.
The bodily process of breathing; the inhalation of air to provide oxygen, and the exhalation of spent air to remove carbon dioxide.

An inclination to undertake a certain kind of work, especially a religious career; often in response to a perceived summons; a calling.
An occupation for which a person is suited, trained or qualified.
A robot or artificial being that is an exact copy of a genuine lifeform, especially a human.
One who replies.
Replicative
A company; troop; a gang.
A slingshot
To cry, weep.
Any of many trees, of genus Eucalyptus, native mainly to Australia.
A greenish colour, like that of a eucalyptus leaves.
A situation which poses an immediate risk and which requires urgent attention.

definitions.definition: The department of a hospital that treats emergencies.
An individual brought in at short notice to replace a member of staff, a player in a sporting team, etc.
The quality of being emergent; sudden or unexpected appearance; an unforeseen occurrence.
An extended adventurous voyage.
An intellectual or spiritual quest.
Thin, woven, gauze-like fabric.
A fine transparent silk material, used for veils, etc.; specifically, cloth interwoven with gold or silver threads, or embossed with figures.
A sheet of absorbent paper, especially one that is made to be used as tissue paper, toilet paper or a handkerchief.
Absorbent paper as material.
A group of cells similar in origin that function together to do a specific job.
Web; texture; complicated fabrication; connected series.

definitions.definition: To form tissue of; to interweave.
The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, and conceptual understanding; the cognitive faculty (uncountable)

definitions.definition: A person who has that faculty to a great degree

definitions.definition: A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
A transparent screen made of glass, located at the front of a vehicle in order to protect its occupants from the wind and weather

definitions.definition: A cover for a microphone to exclude airy noises such as wind and breathing.
To install a windscreen on.
A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift

Either of two species of large amphibious reptile, Alligator mississippiensis or Alligator sinensis, in the genus Alligator within order Crocodilia, which have sharp teeth and very strong jaws and are native to the Americas and China, respectively.

definitions.definition: Dwarf crocodile (Osteolaemus tetraspis)
Any of various machines with strong jaws, one of which opens like the movable jaw of an alligator.
Any of various vehicles that have relatively long, low noses in front of a cab or other, usually windowed, structure.
(of paint or other coatings) To crack in a pattern resembling an alligator's skin.
Used in a common chronometric counting scheme, in which each iteration is sequentially numbered and supposed to be approximately one second in length.
To engage in mock combat on horseback, as two knights in the lists; to tilt.
To engage in verbal sparring over an important issue. (used of two people, both of whom participate more or less equally)
To touch penises while engaging in a sex act, especially oral sex.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of energy, work and heat; the work required to exert a force of one newton for a distance of one metre. Also equal to the energy of one watt of power for a duration of one second. Symbol: J
An archeological or paleontological investigation, or the site where such an investigation is taking place.
A plodding and laborious student.
A thrust; a poke.

A defensive pass of the ball that has been attacked by the opposing team.
A cutting, sarcastic remark.
A rare or interesting vinyl record bought second-hand.

definitions.definition: To get by digging; to take from the ground; often with up.

To investigate, to research, often followed by out or up.

To journey; to wander; to ramble.

(with for) missing one's home and family very much when away; nostalgic

A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A randomly selected target.

A ball which has passed through all the hoops and would go out if it hit the stake but is continued in play; also, the player of such a ball.

definitions.definition: A drawing or picture.

To enter upon some labour or contest; to join in close fight; to contend.

General; covering or encompassing everything.

Venal; corrupt; jobbing.

Endlessness, unlimitedness, absence of a beginning, end or limits to size.

A number which is very large compared to some characteristic number. For example, in optics, an object which is much further away than the focal length of a lens is said to be "at infinity", as the distance of the image from the lens varies very little as the distance increases further.
The symbol ∞.

Forgetfulness; cessation of remembrance of wrong; oblivion.
